key_insight: A pretrained foundation-model MLIP can be adaptively fine-tuned during evolutionary crystal structure prediction to cut DFT labeling cost while still finding stable new phases.
engineering_relevance: This workflow makes high-throughput crystal structure prediction more practical for complex multicomponent materials by reducing expensive ab initio relaxations and focusing labeling on the most informative candidates.
method: Start with a pretrained machine-learning interatomic potential.
Run evolutionary CSP to explore candidate crystal structures efficiently.
Select compact, representative, physically relevant structures for iterative DFT labeling and fine-tune the potential self-consistently.
evidence_signal: On the Ca-Fe-Ni system, the approach reproduced the known low-pressure convex hull and predicted a new compound, Ca6FeNi, that becomes stable above 100 GPa.
watch_item: Performance still depends on the quality of the pretrained model and on whether the adaptive selection policy generalizes well to other chemistries and pressure regimes.
paper_title: Adaptive fine-tuning of foundation models for crystal structure prediction: Discovery of high-pressure phases in the CaFeNi system
